meta.title: About Us | Armature Wealth Office
meta.description: Built for the phase that comes after success. Armature exists for a specific moment in a professional's life.

hero.heading: Built for the phase that comes after success.

origin.heading: The origin is governance, not finance.
paragraphs: Most wealth management firms begin inside the financial industry. Armature did not. It began inside healthcare, where governance failures are measured in human cost. Poor handoffs. Incomplete documentation. Protocols ignored or never written. Training gaps that surfaced only after someone was hurt.

The pattern repeated everywhere: fragmented coordination, unclear accountability, no single point of oversight holding the system together. Leadership that could not see what the frontline already knew. Evidence-based practice that existed on paper but not in execution.

Armature was built because the founder's own financial life revealed the identical pattern. Multiple entities, disconnected advisors, estate plans that contradicted investment structures. All managed by competent professionals, none of whom could see the full picture. The coordination gap was not a service problem. It was a structural one.
What began as a personal operating system became a practice. The pattern is remarkably consistent across high-earning professionals.

items.title: Governance before strategy
items.body: The right structure precedes the right decision.

items.title: Signal before noise
items.body: We reduce what you monitor and elevate what matters.

items.title: Integration before fragmentation
items.body: Every advisor, asset, and entity is held in a single coherent view.

items.title: Continuity before complexity
items.body: Systems are designed to endure. Not to impress.

items.title: Authorship before delegation
items.body: The principal retains authority. The system does the work.

clientProfile.heading: Who we serve
clientProfile.intro: Armature serves a specific client: an accomplished professional with $6M–$15M+ in net worth, whose success has created complexity that has outgrown informal coordination.
signals: Multiple advisory relationships, none of which see the full picture
Real estate, entities, or business interests managed separately
A sense that things are working, but not held together
Growing awareness that the next phase requires more structure
A desire to delegate oversight without losing authority
clientProfile.closing: If this describes your current position, the timing may be right.

closing.heading: This is not a pitch. It is a pattern.
closing.body: Armature exists for a specific moment in a professional’s life. When success has arrived, the complexity has compounded, and the question is no longer ‘how do I grow?’ but ‘how do I hold this together?’ That is when governance matters most.
